# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/http/server.ts";
import { parse } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/url/mod.ts";

serve(async (req) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url, `http://${req.headers.get("host")}`);
  const params = parse(url.search);

  if (url.pathname !== "/api/search") {
    return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  }

  const userId = params.userId;
  const filterParam = params.filter;

  if (!userId || !filterParam) {
    return new Response("Missing required parameters", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const kv = await Deno.openKv();
    const key = ["users", userId, filterParam] as [string, string, string];
    const result = await kv.get(key);

    if (result.value) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result.value), { status: 200 });
    } else {
      return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
});
